jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Car crash aftermath from opening credits
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature exclamation of pain/frustration
jokes.explanation: Iconic character catchphrase that defines Homer's character - perfect economy and integration

jokes.text: Ha-ha!
jokes.punchline: Nelson's signature bullying laugh
jokes.explanation: Another iconic character moment - pure Nelson in two syllables

jokes.text: The experts say that if you want an animal to do something... you should do it yourself first to show 'em how. I'm not going to the bathroom in the backyard.
jokes.punchline: Refusing to follow the advice to its logical conclusion
jokes.explanation: Good setup-punchline structure with absurd logic

jokes.text: [Scoffs] Sorry, Your Majesty.
jokes.setup: Marge refusing to demonstrate bathroom behavior
jokes.punchline: Homer's sarcastic response treating her like royalty
jokes.explanation: Mild sarcasm that fits Homer's character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: [Rattles, Hisses] [Screams] - Aaah! - Oh, Ned, I'm so sorry.
jokes.punchline: Snake literally appears, scaring neighbor Ned
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag with unexpected real snake in yard debris

jokes.text: - Allowance day. Ding, ding, ding, ding, ding. - You don't deserve an allowance.
jokes.punchline: Homer's blunt dismissal
jokes.explanation: Classic parent-child dynamic with Homer's typical bluntness

jokes.text: That's a good suggestion, Mom. - We'll take it under advisement.
jokes.setup: Mom ordering them to clean the yard
jokes.punchline: Kids treating parental command like corporate suggestion
jokes.explanation: Clever role reversal using business speak to deflect parental authority

jokes.text: [Nelson] Ha-
jokes.setup: Homer claiming no one will see him in the backyard
jokes.punchline: Immediately proven wrong by Nelson's appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and irony - Homer's prediction immediately contradicted

jokes.text: [Gasps] Lisa, chop off my hands.
jokes.setup: Looking for way out of yard work
jokes.punchline: Extreme solution of self-mutilation
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation that's perfectly in character for Bart's scheming

jokes.text: No! Then who'd chop off my hands?
jokes.setup: Bart asking Lisa to chop off his hands
jokes.punchline: Lisa's logical concern about the mutual hand-chopping problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist logic - Lisa's practical consideration of the impossible scenario

jokes.text: All right, you chop my hands halfway off, then I'll still have enough strength to chop-
jokes.punchline: Bart's elaborate solution involving partial amputation
jokes.explanation: Continues the absurd escalation with Bart's twisted problem-solving

jokes.text: - What are you doing inside? - Work was hard, so we quit.
jokes.setup: Kids supposed to be working outside
jokes.punchline: Bart's matter-of-fact explanation for giving up
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of child logic - work is hard, therefore quit

jokes.text: Hard work made us quit.
jokes.punchline: Lisa's even more direct restatement
jokes.explanation: Lisa joining Bart's logic with even more economy of words

jokes.text: Okay. We get our room and board free anyway. - And Santy Claus provides the rest.
jokes.punchline: Kids pointing out they don't really need money
jokes.explanation: Kids turning the tables on parental leverage with sound economic reasoning

jokes.text: It's carnival time! Here's money, kids. Take what you need.
jokes.setup: Marge about to enforce chores for allowance
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately undermining discipline with carnival excitement
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer parenting fail - completely undercutting Marge's authority

jokes.text: I wanna go on the Yard Work Simulator!
jokes.punchline: Bart wanting to do as entertainment what he refused to do as chores
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic observation about how context changes perception of the same activity

jokes.text: But when I ask you to do yard work- Oh.
jokes.punchline: Marge realizing the irony mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat showing Marge's dawning realization of the contradiction

jokes.text: Heart condition? Get out of here.
jokes.setup: Being told he has a heart condition
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete denial of medical reality
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer denial of obvious facts, perfectly in character

jokes.text: You had a quadruple bypass. You nearly died. Don't you remember?
jokes.punchline: Reveal of extremely serious recent medical procedure
jokes.explanation: Good escalation revealing how serious Homer's denial is

jokes.text: Obviously I don't.
jokes.setup: Marge asking if he remembers his surgery
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact admission of not remembering
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response showing Homer's complete lack of concern

jokes.text: - No offense. - None taken.
jokes.punchline: Carny completely unfazed by the insult
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing carny's thick skin and self-awareness

jokes.text: He's not just some guy, Marge. He's a carny, and part of a noble tradition. Carnies built this country- the carnival part of it anyway.
jokes.setup: Homer defending carnies as noble tradition builders
jokes.punchline: Qualifier that they only built the carnival part
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection joke with perfect qualifier that deflates the grandiose claim

jokes.text: And though they may be ratlike in appearance... they are truly kings among men.
jokes.punchline: Calling them kings among men despite the insult
jokes.explanation: Good contradiction showing Homer's confused logic

jokes.text: - Okay, let's go, fatty. - Yes, sir.
jokes.setup: Homer just calling carnies 'kings among men'
jokes.punchline: Carny immediately insulting Homer, who accepts it happily
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic reversal - Homer's respect is met with contempt he doesn't mind

jokes.text: - [All] Ow! - [Homer] Ow! My heart!
jokes.punchline: Homer specifically yelling about his heart condition
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the heart condition setup with predictable but satisfying payoff

jokes.text: Do you dare enter the Screamatorium of Dr. Frightmarestein?
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top combination name 'Frightmarestein'
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay combining multiple scary elements into one ridiculous name

jokes.text: [Woman Screaming] [Tape Rewinding] - [Button Clicks] - [Woman Screaming]
jokes.punchline: Revealed to be just a tape recorder on loop
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag exposing the cheap carnival tricks

jokes.text: This isn't very scary.
jokes.punchline: Bart's underwhelmed assessment
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deadpan observation of the obvious

jokes.text: Um, I think that one's broken.
jokes.punchline: Homer thinking it's broken rather than just cheap
jokes.explanation: Homer's naive interpretation of obviously bad attraction effects

jokes.text: That was just confusing.
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact critique
jokes.explanation: Lisa's intellectual response to carnival nonsense

jokes.text: [Sputtering] Why does this always happen when I stand here?
jokes.setup: Person in dunk tank getting splashed
jokes.punchline: Wondering why it always happens in that specific spot
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with character's obliviousness to obvious cause

jokes.text: What is it about this exact spot? [Sputtering] It's happening again!
jokes.punchline: Immediately getting dunked again
jokes.explanation: Good timing with immediate repeat of the thing he's questioning

jokes.text: Hey, saw-top. Want your dad to win you a TV?
jokes.punchline: Calling Bart 'saw-top' referring to his spiky hair
jokes.explanation: Good character detail with carny's colorful slang for Bart's distinctive hair

jokes.text: Ooh, that was close. Wow. What a scam.
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately recognizing and accepting it's rigged
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual acceptance of being scammed shows his good nature

jokes.text: Aw, listen, fella. I got a son of my own. Here.
jokes.setup: Homer failing at rigged game and disappointing Bart
jokes.punchline: Carny showing unexpected kindness
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing unexpected humanity in the carny

jokes.text: [Sniffles] A novelty comb. Kings among men.
jokes.setup: Getting consolation prize of cheap comb
jokes.punchline: Homer reaffirming his earlier praise despite the tiny gesture
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Homer maintaining his opinion despite minimal evidence

jokes.text: and was the first automobile to come with Fahrvergnügen.
jokes.punchline: Anachronistic reference to 1990s Volkswagen ad campaign
jokes.explanation: Clever anachronistic joke mixing historical figure with contemporary ad slogan

jokes.text: Wow, it's 'Euro-rific.'
jokes.punchline: Lisa's made-up positive adjective combining 'Euro' and 'terrific'
jokes.explanation: Lisa's innocent enthusiasm creates ironic contrast with dark subject matter

jokes.text: There are some who say Hitler's skeleton is in the trunk. Others say just a spare tire. I'm a busy man, so I haven't checked yet.
jokes.setup: Mysterious contents of Hitler's car trunk
jokes.punchline: Too busy to check if it's Hitler's remains or just a tire
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan delivery treating monumental historical discovery as minor inconvenience

jokes.text: Hmm. One of these must activate the flamethrower.
jokes.punchline: Assumption that Hitler's car would have weapons
jokes.explanation: Good setup for upcoming chaos, showing Bart's mischievous curiosity

jokes.text: Out of my way! I'm Hitler!
jokes.setup: Bart in the car causing chaos
jokes.punchline: Bart embracing the Hitler persona while driving recklessly
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with Bart's childish interpretation of historical villain

jokes.text: You wrecked Hitler's car. What did he ever do to you?
jokes.punchline: Carny defending Hitler as if he's the victim
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly subversive line treating Hitler as wronged party - excellent dark comedy

jokes.text: Hey, I thought I was your main attraction.
jokes.setup: Carny calling Hitler's car the main attraction
jokes.punchline: Side show performer feeling replaced
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing carnival hierarchy and jealousy

jokes.text: Hey, come on. He doesn't have any money. Look at his clothes.
jokes.punchline: Homer pointing out obvious poverty via appearance
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor with Homer's practical assessment

jokes.text: You gonna get drunk and start a lot of trouble? - Sure, anything you want.
jokes.setup: Carny asking if Lisa will cause trouble
jokes.punchline: Lisa eagerly agreeing to bad behavior
jokes.explanation: Excellent character humor with Lisa's innocent enthusiasm for corruption

jokes.text: We just saw the most amazing camel. It was wearing a hat.
jokes.punchline: Marge's amazement at mundane detail
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's simple pleasures and innocent wonder

jokes.text: Son, if you don't finish your cotton candy... you won't get your snow cone.
jokes.setup: Normal parent threatening to withhold treats
jokes.punchline: Withholding one treat for not finishing another treat
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer parenting logic - all sugar, no actual nutrition or discipline

jokes.text: Hey, you lost your money fair and square. I didn't scam nobody.
jokes.punchline: Carny claiming fairness while holding scam stick
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing carny's twisted sense of fairness

jokes.text: Well, in that case, let me show you how I scammed you.
jokes.setup: Just claiming he didn't scam anyone
jokes.punchline: Immediately offering to demonstrate how he did scam them
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection with Cooder immediately contradicting himself

jokes.text: - Ugh. - Oh, don't worry. That's just to scare off thieves.
jokes.punchline: Claiming it's intentionally bad for security
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about carnival food quality with practical justification

jokes.text: That's the carny code. - Did you hear that, Bart? The carny code.
jokes.punchline: Homer's reverent repetition of the concept
jokes.explanation: Sets up important recurring element with Homer's naive fascination

jokes.text: So what's our first job, huh? Test the roller coaster? - Fry up some cheese? - Hose down the freaks?
jokes.punchline: Increasingly inappropriate job suggestions ending with dehumanizing freaks
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer and Bart's ignorant enthusiasm

jokes.text: Maybe if you didn't go to the bathroom so much, you wouldn't be so small.
jokes.punchline: Bart's absurd theory about size and bathroom habits
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart logic - completely wrong but delivered with confidence

jokes.text: All right. Now, this geek bit is pretty straightforward. You just bite the heads off the chickens, take a bow.
jokes.punchline: Treating grotesque act as simple performance
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy treating bizarre act as routine work instruction

jokes.text: - Uh, Bart? - Yeah, Dad? - Do I like chicken? - Does it matter?
jokes.punchline: Homer's existential confusion about his food preferences
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment with Homer's completely wrong priorities

jokes.text: - you're the luckiest chicken in the world. - [Squawking]
jokes.setup: Chicken about to have head bitten off
jokes.punchline: Homer calling it lucky to be spared
jokes.explanation: Good dark irony about the chicken's 'luck' in avoiding horrible fate

jokes.text: You see, the trick here is the rings won't fit over the good prizes.
jokes.punchline: Openly explaining the scam method
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor exposing carnival scam mechanics

jokes.text: slang from the '30s that no one uses anymore. - Like 'rubes'? - Now you're on the trolley.
jokes.punchline: Immediately using more outdated slang in response
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor with self-aware demonstration of the concept being explained

jokes.text: Uh, yes. How do we handle refunds? - Uh, I'm gonna give this to you.
jokes.punchline: Cooder giving weapon instead of explanation
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing how carnies 'handle' customer complaints

jokes.text: Lookee, lookee. Hey, hey. Wocka, wocka. I got rings and you want 'em.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to be a carnival barker
jokes.punchline: Terrible attempt at carnival patter
jokes.explanation: Good character humor with Homer's awful carnival barker impression

jokes.text: Hey, there's a sucker.
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately applying it to identify customers
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart quickly adopting carny mentality

jokes.text: Get on the trolley.
jokes.punchline: Bart using the outdated phrase he just learned
jokes.explanation: Nice callback with Bart immediately using the vintage slang

jokes.text: Hey, hot pockets. Win something for your girlfriend?
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate nickname and assumption about relationship
jokes.explanation: Mild humor with Homer's awkward attempt at carny flirtation

jokes.text: All right, Simpson, be honest with me. Is it actually possible to win this game? - If I like you, it is.
jokes.punchline: Homer's completely honest corruption
jokes.explanation: Excellent honesty about corruption with Homer's matter-of-fact delivery

jokes.text: You're failing, Seymour. What is it about you and failure?
jokes.setup: Skinner trying to win game for his mother
jokes.punchline: Agnes's brutal psychological assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Agnes's cruel psychological dominance

jokes.text: If only we weren't tied down with a family.
jokes.punchline: Wishing his family didn't exist
jokes.explanation: Dark character humor with Homer's casual dismissal of family responsibilities

jokes.text: where people throw ducks at balloons and nothing's the way it seems.
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical game concept with philosophical undertone
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor mixing nonsense with deeper meaning about carnival deception

jokes.text: [Laughs] And how.
jokes.punchline: Homer proudly confirming the corruption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - completely missing the threat and bragging instead

jokes.text: Uh, let me put it this way. I'm looking for my friend Bill. - Have you seen any Bills around here? - No. He's Bart.
jokes.punchline: Homer completely misunderstanding and correcting the name
jokes.explanation: Excellent misunderstanding humor with Homer's literal interpretation

jokes.text: Listen carefully, and watch me wink as I speak, okay? - Okay. - The guy I'm really looking for-wink- is Mr. Bribe-wink, wink.
jokes.setup: Wiggum trying to be more obvious about bribery
jokes.punchline: Explicitly explaining the winking and saying 'Mr. Bribe'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing increasing desperation to communicate obvious bribe request

jokes.text: It's a ringtoss game.
jokes.punchline: Homer still completely missing the point
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing with Homer's oblivious response to increasingly obvious corruption

jokes.text: Why didn't you bribe 'em? - I was trying to, but the opportunity never came up.
jokes.setup: Cooder asking why Homer didn't bribe the cops
jokes.punchline: Homer claiming no opportunity despite obvious attempts
jokes.explanation: Great callback showing Homer's complete obliviousness to the obvious opportunity

jokes.text: Did you call me, Spud? [Groans]
jokes.punchline: Person actually living in the game getting hit
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual gag revealing someone actually lives in the Whack-A-Mole game

jokes.text: Look at 'em, Bart. If a carny can wind up homeless, it can happen to anyone.
jokes.punchline: Homer treating carnies as the baseline for housing security
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic logic treating the already homeless as example of housing vulnerability

jokes.text: - Why did you shudder just now, Mom? - I don't know.
jokes.punchline: Marge's involuntary physical reaction of dread
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Marge's subconscious awareness of impending disaster

jokes.text: It must have taken you years to win all this stuff.
jokes.punchline: Assuming all possessions come from carnival games
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing carny's limited worldview

jokes.text: I can uninge all my joints at once. - [Bones Cracking]
jokes.punchline: Gross physical contortion
jokes.explanation: Standard gross-out physical comedy typical of carnival performers

jokes.text: - Doing anything tonight, blue eyes? - Ew.
jokes.punchline: Lisa's disgusted reaction
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable cringe humor that relies on inappropriate behavior for shock value

jokes.text: Once their résumé gets out, they'll have all kinds of offers.
jokes.setup: Marge asking how long carnies will stay
jokes.punchline: Homer's optimistic view of carny job prospects
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Homer's naive optimism about unemployable guests

jokes.text: The older one can pull out his left eye.
jokes.punchline: Treating gross physical deformity as job skill
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor treating bizarre ability as marketable skill

jokes.text: Oh, hell, the Cooders have been carnies ever since we came here in 1620... clinging to the side of the Mayflower.
jokes.punchline: Absurd claim of arriving as Mayflower stowaways
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist backstory mixing American history with carnival life

jokes.text: Aw, aren't you sweet? Hey, my pearls. - Spud taught me that.
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was pickpocketing training
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection turning sweet moment into criminal training revelation

jokes.text: 'Cause we just happen to have some tickets.
jokes.setup: Offering glass-bottom boat ride as repayment
jokes.punchline: Convenient excuse to get family out of house
jokes.explanation: Sets up the plot mechanism but not particularly funny on its own

jokes.text: Now who's the filthy sleazebag, huh, Marge?
jokes.punchline: Homer vindicated about carny character despite obvious setup
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's premature vindication

jokes.text: That's our sister ship, the Athena. It went down with 88 souls just last week.
jokes.setup: Tour guide pointing out underwater wreck
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning recent disaster with body count
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with matter-of-fact delivery of horrible information

jokes.text: They're the nicest of all the transients you've ever brought home.
jokes.punchline: Revealing Homer regularly brings home homeless strangers
jokes.explanation: Great character revelation about Homer's pattern of bringing home strangers

jokes.text: Check this out! - What's going on? - Cooder!
jokes.punchline: House completely transformed/barricaded
jokes.explanation: Good visual revelation of the carnies' takeover

jokes.text: It's ours now. Tell us where the extra sheets are and get off our property.
jokes.punchline: Demanding household information while evicting owners
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment mixing practical needs with hostile takeover

jokes.text: You can't do this to me. What about the carny code?
jokes.punchline: Appealing to code he believed in
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback showing Homer's naive faith being crushed

jokes.text: [Cooder, Spud Laughing]
jokes.punchline: Carnies laughing at his naivety
jokes.explanation: Effective cruel reaction beat showing complete dismissal of Homer's faith

jokes.text: Well, well, look who's here. Mr. No-Bribe.
jokes.punchline: Cop still bitter about failed bribe attempt
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Wiggum's petty corruption and grudge-holding

jokes.text: I hope everyone likes the red things that grow on this tree.
jokes.setup: Family forced to forage for food
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete ignorance about basic fruits
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's disconnection from nature

jokes.text: - [Cooder] That Urkel's a hoot. - Listen to 'em.
jokes.punchline: Homer's bitter observation of their comfort
jokes.explanation: Mild humor with dated cultural reference and Homer's resentment

jokes.text: You'd better not be in my ass groove!
jokes.punchline: Homer's attachment to his couch indentation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment combining Homer's laziness with territorial instincts

jokes.text: It took me years to forge that groove.
jokes.setup: Homer upset about his couch groove
jokes.punchline: Treating body-shaped indentation as crafted achievement
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation treating accidental body impression as deliberate craftsmanship

jokes.text: Hey, look at me. I'm a millionaire. Wow, Dad! You look likeJames Bond.
jokes.setup: Homer wearing fancy clothing from their house
jokes.punchline: Bart's admiring comparison to James Bond
jokes.explanation: Basic visual gag with family's positive attitude despite circumstances. Functional but not remarkable.

jokes.text: Which one? 'Precious Moments' or 'Treasured Memories'? - Quit yer yappir. I'm freezir.
jokes.punchline: Casual choice between family memories and warmth
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about destroying precious family memories for warmth

jokes.text: I say we set fire to the house. Kill 'em that way.
jokes.setup: Family discussing how to get house back
jokes.punchline: Bart's extreme violent solution
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with Bart's typically violent problem-solving approach

jokes.text: - Well, if we did set fire to the house- - No fires!
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately trying to continue fire discussion
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction showing Bart's persistence with bad ideas

jokes.text: Just look at the way they sucker regular folk with those crooked games.
jokes.punchline: Describing exactly what happened to him as proof of their skill
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment with Homer inadvertently describing his own victimization

jokes.text: [Gasps] That's it. Fire!
jokes.setup: Homer having inspiration about carnival games
jokes.punchline: Immediately jumping to Bart's fire solution
jokes.explanation: Callback to Bart's idea with Homer's typically impulsive adoption

jokes.text: If I can throw this hula hoop onto the chimney, you'll give us our house back. If I miss, I'll sign the deed over to you.
jokes.punchline: Wagering house ownership on impossible carnival game
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Homer's terrible gambling instincts and overconfidence

jokes.text: - Homer, no! - Oh, you'll never make it, Dad.
jokes.punchline: Family's complete lack of faith in Homer
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamic showing their realistic assessment of Homer's abilities

jokes.text: [Grunts] Now! - What the-
jokes.punchline: Unexpected success through family teamwork
jokes.explanation: Good visual payoff with unexpected success through teamwork rather than skill

jokes.text: That Homer fella grifted you good, Dad. - Well, there's no shame in beir beaten by the best.
jokes.punchline: Cooder's gracious acceptance treating Homer as master con artist
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic moment with carny respecting Homer's accidental con artistry

jokes.text: But he didn't seem all that- - We were beaten by the best, boy.
jokes.punchline: Cooder insisting on maintaining the illusion of being outclassed
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Cooder's pride requiring face-saving interpretation

jokes.text: Way to go, Dad. You actually outwitted someone.
jokes.punchline: Bart's surprise that Homer showed any intelligence
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamic with Bart's backhanded praise highlighting Homer's usual incompetence

jokes.text: Poor guys. Hey, what if we let 'em stay here for just a few days?
jokes.punchline: Immediately wanting to help the people who just betrayed him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment showing his complete inability to learn from experience

jokes.text: Aw, they mushed it all out of shape.
jokes.punchline: Discovering his precious indentation has been ruined
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Homer's earlier concern with his worst fears realized

jokes.text: All right. Better put on a pot of coffee, Marge. This could take a while.
jokes.setup: Homer discovering his ruined couch groove
jokes.punchline: Preparing for extended restoration project
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's priorities and dedication to comfort over everything else
